weather_line: Southwest winds, secondary channel running · ducks oriented correctly

stories.headline: They Go; the Southwest Quadrant Receives Them; the Chart Is Settled in the Only Way That Counts
body: A departure time was named at seven forty-two this morning — not proposed, not floated, named — and by eight o'clock the enchanted Jeep was pointed southwest with both brass ducks level on the shelf and the chart folded to the conditional quadrant uppermost. Steve drove. Christine navigated. Neither mentioned the ink difference, the November margins, or the secondary channel, because all of that was already settled and what remained was simply the terrain.

The southwest quadrant, for its part, was exactly what the chart had been carefully not saying: steep in the first third, loose in the second, and then — past the channel crossing, which was running at precisely the depth the ghost line had implied — open. A long shelf of exposed sandstone above the tree canopy, southwest-facing, mid-morning light arriving at an angle the chart had no notation for because no notation would have been adequate.

Steve reached nine and a half out of ten, which Christine accepted as fair and possibly generous to the chart. The secondary channel was forded without incident. The brass ducks have been moved to a new position on the shelf — forward, slightly south — which Steve has not explained and Christine has not asked him to. The Jeep returned satisfied. The southwest quadrant is now settled. A new section of chart is not yet on the table, but the table is not full.

stories.headline: Saturday Is Not a Vault Day; the Vault Requests Have Come In Anyway; One Has Been Answered on Principle
body: Saturday is, by longstanding agreement, not a working day at Gringotts, a fact that Christine has noted in writing twice and the goblins have acknowledged in the same correspondence in which they attached three follow-up items marked Urgent (Ambient).

One inquiry — from a Mr. Thaddeus Flume of Vault 512, concerning an automatic interest adjustment he described as 'irregular' and the goblins described as 'contractually foreseeable' — was answered at nine-fifteen from the trail, in three sentences, via enchanted post. Mr. Flume has replied to say it was very clear. The duty goblin has not read the reply yet, it being Saturday, but is expected to find it significant when Monday arrives.

The commendation portrait — brass-framed, third pillar left, Accounts Liaison Wing — was not passed by anyone today. This is the first Saturday on record in which it was not passed. The duty goblin assigned to count has logged this as 'Passings: 0 (Saturday, conditions apply)' and considers the matter properly documented.

stories.headline: Ministry Issues Weekend Bulletin Confirming the Week Is Over; Declines to Characterise What Follows
body: The Department of Magical Coordination has issued its Saturday bulletin, which runs to four paragraphs and confirms, in descending order of confidence: that the week has concluded, that the weekend has begun, that conditions are 'broadly ambient,' and that a further bulletin will follow on Monday to clarify whether the weekend was, in retrospect, what they expected.

Buried in the third paragraph, beside a footnote concerning the correct disposal of outdated Floo permits, is a reference to a glowing briefcase that passed through Ministry security at eleven-oh-three on Friday evening. The security log describes it as 'Unattended (Luminous)' and notes it was collected by a short man in a suit who did not give his name but seemed extremely motivated. The fourth paragraph does not return to this. Neither does the Ministry.

stories.headline: Saturday at the Household: A Vacancy Is Identified, Contested, and Filled; Biscuit Inventory Holds — for Now
body: With Christine and Steve absent for the morning's expedition, the household convened informally. Lucy conducted a full audit of the biscuit situation, produced no findings, and retired to the south-facing windowsill, where she remained until the Jeep was heard returning, at which point she relocated to the entry rug and arranged herself to suggest she had been there all morning.

Ellie spent forty minutes in productive engagement with her reflection in the oven door, which she has now decided is 'a different dog who lives inside the kitchen.' She is not troubled by this. She finds it companionable.

Jersey identified the warmest available surface at eight-fifteen — the armchair vacated by Steve at departure — and occupied it with such thoroughness that by noon it was unclear whether the armchair was a chair that contained Jersey or a Jersey-shaped arrangement that had once been a chair. He yielded it upon Steve's return as a gesture of goodwill. Steve thanked him. Jersey considered this sufficient.

familiars_corner: Lucy: conducted a full biscuit audit in Christine and Steve's absence, found nothing, filed no report, and was on the entry rug before the Jeep reached the drive — expression neutral, alibi complete.
Ellie: has made a friend who lives in the oven door and appears whenever she approaches; she finds the friendship reliable, low-maintenance, and asks nothing difficult of either party.
Jersey: held the armchair from eight-fifteen to twelve-forty, yielded it voluntarily upon Steve's return, and considers the morning a well-administered success.
founding_editor_note: That shelf view sounds about right — some of the best ones don't have a notation for the light, and that's not the chart's fault, that's just how it works. Good day's work, all around. — D.

in_brief.sport: Southwest quadrant sandstone shelf rated nine and a half out of ten; steepness in the first third considered character-building rather than a deduction.
in_brief.forecast: Sunday: conditions ambient, table not yet full, next chart arriving on its own schedule — probably sooner than later.

storyline_updates.title: Passable (Conditional)
storyline_updates.summary: Resolved Saturday morning: departure named at seven forty-two, southwest quadrant visited, secondary channel forded at the implied depth, sandstone shelf reached. Steve rated nine and a half. The brass ducks moved forward and slightly south — new position, unexplained. The chart is settled. The table is not yet full but has space. Arc closed with full honours. Four arcs total now resolved across the run.

storyline_updates.title: The Briefcase Returns
storyline_updates.summary: New arc opening quietly: a glowing briefcase logged as 'Unattended (Luminous)' passed through Ministry security at eleven-oh-three Friday evening and was collected by a short, unnamed man in a suit who appeared extremely motivated. The Ministry's Saturday bulletin referenced it once and did not return to the subject. Day 2 should establish where the briefcase went — whether it surfaces in Diagon Alley, at Gringotts, or somewhere altogether less expected — and who, if anyone, is looking for it.
